content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Each node has a value; let's denote the value of node iii by viv_ivi‚Äč. Chef likes to travel in this tree. For each pair of nodes (u,v)(u, v)(u,v), he defines the score of the path from uuu to vvv in the following way:


Suppose that the simple path from node uuu to node vvv is a sequence of nodes u=a0,a1,‚Ä¶,aL=vu = a_0, a_1, \ldots, a_L = vu=a0‚Äč,a1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aL‚Äč=v, where LLL is the length of this path.
Let's denote the values of the nodes on this path by a sequence A0,A1,‚Ä¶,ALA_0, A_1, \ldots, A_LA0‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AL‚Äč, where for each valid iii, Ai=vaiA_i = v_{a_i}Ai‚Äč=vai‚Äč‚Äč.
Next, let's denote the suffix sums of the sequence AAA by S0,S1,‚Ä¶,SLS_0, S_1, \ldots, S_LS0‚Äč,S1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SL‚Äč, i.e. for each valid iii, Si=‚ąĎj=iLAjS_i = \sum_{j=i}^L A_jSi‚Äč=‚ąĎj=iL‚ÄčAj‚Äč.
The score f(u,v)f(u, v)f(u,v) of the path from node uuu to node vvv is the sum of all these suffix sums, i.e. f(u,v)=‚ąĎi=0LSif(u, v) = \sum_{i=0}^L S_if(u,v)=‚ąĎi=0L‚ÄčSi‚Äč.
Chef wants to choose some nodes uuu and vvv (u‚Č†vu \neq vuÓÄ†=v) and travel along the simple path from uuu to vvv. However, he can treat it as several consecutive paths, which affects the score of the resulting trip. Specifically, if the simple path from node uuu to node vvv is a sequence of nodes u=a0,a1,‚Ä¶,aL=vu = a_0, a_1, \ldots, a_L = vu=a0‚Äč,a1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aL‚Äč=v, then Chef may choose any non-negative integer mmm and any sequence k1,k2,‚Ä¶,kmk_1, k_2, \ldots, k_mk1‚Äč,k2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,km‚Äč such that 1‚Č§k1<k2<‚Ä¶km<L1 \le k_1 \lt k_2 \lt \ldots k_m \lt L1‚Č§k1‚Äč<k2‚Äč<‚Ä¶km‚Äč<L; then, the score of the trip from uuu to vvv is f(u,ak1)+f(ak1,ak2)+‚Ä¶+f(akm,v)f(u, a_{k_1}) + f(a_{k_1}, a_{k_2}) + \ldots + f(a_{k_m}, v)f(u,ak1‚Äč‚Äč)+f(ak1‚Äč‚Äč,ak2‚Äč‚Äč)+‚Ä¶+f(akm‚Äč‚Äč,v). Specifically, if m=0m=0m=0, the score of the trip is simply f(u,v)f(u, v)f(u,v).

Find the maximum possible score of Chef's trip if the nodes uuu and vvv (such that u‚Č†vu \neq vuÓÄ†=v) and the sequence kkk are chosen optimally.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103

`Explanation**Example case 1:** We have f(1,2)=‚ąí6f(1, 2) = -6f(1,2)=‚ąí6, f(2,1)=‚ąí18f(2, 1) = -18f(2,1)=‚ąí18, f(1,3)=‚ąí2f(1, 3) = -2f(1,3)=‚ąí2, f(3,1)=‚ąí16f(3, 1) = -16f(3,1)=‚ąí16, f(2,3)=‚ąí6f(2, 3) = -6f(2,3)=‚ąí6 and f(3,2)=‚ąí10f(3, 2) = -10f(3,2)=‚ąí10. A trip from node 222 to node 333 can be split into the paths 2‚Üí1‚Üí32 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 32‚Üí1‚Üí3 with score ‚ąí20-20‚ąí20, and a trip from node 333 to node 222 can be split into the paths 3‚Üí1‚Üí23 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 23‚Üí1‚Üí2 with score ‚ąí22-22‚ąí22. Each trip can also be treated as one path. The maximum score is ‚ąí2-2‚ąí2 for the trip from node 111 to node 333.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vNv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Nv1‚Äč,v2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,vN‚Äč.
N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb denoting that nodes aaa and bbb are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the maximum possible score.
